title: company spawn workstream department role lane
purpose: F2.2 defines company spawn workstream department role lane for the agent-driven expert system. Organization — template-packs as whole-company ceiling.
scope: Owns `F2.2` only; siblings under `F2` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).

Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §8 — Branch F — Organization plane (template-packs = ceiling)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#8-branch-f-organization-plane-template-packs-ceiling).
behavior_steps: Each promotion item in `state.platform.promotion_queue` carries age; after S0 preflight the conductor compares oldest backlog age to `drain_policy.max_platform_backlog_age` from the active pack ([D5.1](D5.1-configure-task-level-params-only.md)).

When oldest backlog exceeds the cap, force-drain policy schedules platform turns until age falls below the threshold or the queue empties—even if [D3.1](D3.1-1-platform-turn-per-k-product-turns.md) K-step cadence would defer the next platform drain.

Force drain is the adversarial-review backstop across D-branch scheduling: it prevents promotion debt from rotting during long product-only sprints without overriding active product evidence gates or clearing H2 blockers.
Partial promotion with re-enqueue resets item age; marking promotion done without passing [D6](D6-index.md) checklist fails closed and reopens the queue item at H2.

If force drain would starve evidence-blocked product work, the operator tunes age and force intensity per pack, or [D3.3](D3.3-priority-cut-product-blocked-missing-tool.md) suspends drain until the missing artifact class is enqueued.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Pack role handoff while lane lease held â€” complete-work-order releases lease before role switch.
Edge case `F2.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `F2.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Per-role allowed_reads in lane.json work orders.
Validate `F2.2` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `F2.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `F2.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: F2.2 defines company spawn workstream department role lane for the agent-driven expert system. Organization — template-packs as whole-company ceiling. In practice, the system each promotion item in `state.platform.promotion_queue` carries age; after s0 preflight the conductor compares oldest backlog age to `drain_policy.max_platform_backlog_age` from the active pack ([d5.1](d5.1-configure-task-level-params-only.md)), then when oldest backlog exceeds the cap, force-drain policy schedules platform turns until age falls below the threshold or the queue empties—even if [d3.1](d3.1-1-platform-turn-per-k-product-turns.md) k-step cadence would defer the next platform drain.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-f 2026-06-28 -->

After pack bind, program-scoper spawns program workstreams mapped to departments or role lanes—each lane gets lane.json leases, work orders, and spawn_policy compatible with integration manifest. Department boundaries align with pack roles ([F1.2](F1.2-pack-roles---yaml.md)); parallel lanes require manifest gate clearance ([F1.4](F1.4-pack-manifest-md-integration-contracts.md)).

orchestrate-program spawns workers only for ready lanes with valid leases ([C4.1](C4.1-workstreams-lane-json-leases.md)). Spawn without manifest approval violates program pipeline order. Lane spawn is S0 where lane scripts exist; otherwise conductor invokes program-scoper skill with pack context.
